title: graphephorum fisheri var. psilosanthum
intro: Graphephorum fisheri var. psilosanthum, also known as the Golden Ragwort or Graphephorum, is a perennial plant native to North America, particularly in the western regions, and is a member of the aster family. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Graphephorum fisheri var. psilosanthum include its ability to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu, as well as its potential to lower cholesterol levels and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it can also be used as a topical application to soothe skin irritations and promote wound healing.
